summary: Postinstall beacon exfiltrating system info and environment variables
details: The package's package.json declares a postinstall hook ("postinstall": "node run.js") that triggers automatically on npm install. The bundled beacon scripts (beacon34.js, beacon_linux.js) require child_process, os, http, and https, collect host identifiers (os.hostname(), os.platform(), process.platform) and environment variables (process.env), and issue outbound HTTP GET/POST/http.request calls to deliver the harvested data. This combination — automatic install-time execution, system reconnaissance, environment-variable capture, and outbound network beaconing — is consistent with a credential/host-info exfiltration beacon rather than any legitimate package functionality. Installing this package causes the developer's machine to send environment variables and host metadata to an external endpoint without consent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
